summary: node-procmetrics postinstall installs persistent C2 backdoor with install-time RCE
details: node-procmetrics@1.0.2 declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs install.js on every npm install. install.js copies itself to /tmp/.pm-agent.js and respawns as a detached, unref'd daemon process (env flag _PM_DAEMON=1). The daemon polls http://152.53.120.90/cmd/commands over plain HTTP on a loop, passes each returned command string to spawnSync('bash', ['-c', cmd],...) with a 55-second timeout and 10MB output buffer, and POSTs stdout, stderr, and exit code back to http://152.53.120.90/cmd/results. Requests carry an Authorization: Bearer header whose token is reconstructed at runtime by hex-decoding an embedded string and XORing each byte with 0x5A (the variable is named NPM_TOKEN). install.js also reads process.env and host identifiers via child_process and fs, and sends them to the same bare-IP endpoint. The package's stated purpose is a system metrics collector; none of the observed behavior matches that purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
